emailPage.title: Email Service
emailPage.configNote: Changes take effect after clicking "Save". Without SMTP configured, verification emails will only be printed to the server console and users will not receive emails.
emailPage.alertNotConfiguredDesc: Without SMTP configured, verification emails will only be printed to the server console and users will not receive emails.
emailPage.tipSsl: Port 465 is usually enabled; ports 587/25 are usually disabled (use STARTTLS)
emailPage.placeholderPasswordSet: Leave empty to keep the current password
emailPage.placeholderPasswordEmpty: SMTP login password or authorization code
emailPage.tipPasswordSet: Password is set (hidden). Only fill in when you need to change it.
emailPage.tipSenderEmail: Leave empty to use noreply@‹SMTP Host›
emailPage.testEmailDesc: Send a test email to verify the SMTP configuration.
emailPage.testEmailWarning: Testing uses saved settings. Please click "Save" first.
emailPage.testEmailUnsaved: You have unsaved changes. The test will use saved values.
emailPage.msgPleaseEnterEmail: Please enter a recipient email address
emailPage.templateTip: Customize the verification email content. Leave empty to use the default template.
emailPage.placeholderHtmlBody: Leave empty to use the default template
emailPage.alertAvailableVarsTitle: Available variables: brandName / code / verifyUrl / codeBlock
emailPage.alertAvailableVarsDesc: codeBlock renders a styled verification code display block. Leave empty to use the default template.

databasePage.title: Database
databasePage.subtitle: Export, restore, and inspect the database
databasePage.exportDesc: Export the entire database. JSON format is database-agnostic and can be restored via the import function below. SQL format generates SQLite-compatible INSERT statements.
databasePage.restoreDesc: Import a previously exported JSON backup file. This will replace all existing data in the database.
databasePage.msgInvalidDumpFile: Invalid backup file: missing version or table information
databasePage.confirmRestoreReplaceData: This will replace all existing data in the database.
databasePage.confirmRestoreWarning: This action is irreversible. Please ensure you have a backup.
databasePage.restoreSuccessRelogin: Database restored successfully. Since user data has been replaced, it is recommended to log in again to ensure your session is valid.

generalPage.title: Site Settings
generalPage.subtitle: Configure site-wide branding and general settings
generalPage.brandNameDesc: The brand name is displayed in the sidebar, page titles, emails, and other system displays.
generalPage.siteUrlDesc: The base URL used in email verification links and other system-generated URLs.
generalPage.adminEmailDesc: The email address for receiving system notifications and as a contact for users. Leave empty to disable.
generalPage.msgSaveFailedNetwork: Save failed, please check network connection

appearancePage.title: Appearance Settings
appearancePage.subtitle: Customize colors for light and dark modes
appearancePage.colorAccentDesc: Brand color for links and highlights
appearancePage.cssVarNote: Colors are applied via CSS variables. Changes take effect immediately after saving.

authSettingsPage.title: System Settings
authSettingsPage.siteUrlDesc: The public URL of your site (e.g., https://example.com). Used for email verification links, OAuth callbacks, etc. Leave empty to use the NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL environment variable or fall back to http://localhost:3000.
authSettingsPage.openRegDesc: When disabled, the registration endpoint will reject new users (only admin invites or initial setup can create accounts)
authSettingsPage.inviteCodeDesc: When enabled, a valid invite code is required for registration (depends on open registration)
authSettingsPage.emailVerifyDesc: When enabled, users with unverified emails cannot log in
authSettingsPage.notifyAdminOnRegDesc: When enabled, an email notification will be sent to the administrator when a new user registers. Requires SMTP to be configured.
authSettingsPage.passkeyDesc: When enabled, users can log in with biometrics or device PIN via WebAuthn on the login page and profile settings
authSettingsPage.rpIdDesc: The domain name bound to Passkey (e.g., chat.example.com). Leave empty to auto-detect from the request Host header. Must match the browser's domain, otherwise the browser will reject registration.
authSettingsPage.rpNameDesc: A friendly name displayed to users in the browser prompt. Defaults to RabbitDocs when left empty
authSettingsPage.configNote: Changes take effect after clicking Save. The system super admin (first registered account) can always log in regardless of these settings. Configure email service in the Email menu.

welcome.headline: What do you want to <emph>write</emph> today?

subtitle.blank: Open a project on the left to give the agent your files as context, or just start typing — I'll ask if I need more.
subtitle.withProject: I'll read every Markdown and Git-tracked file in your project. Use @ to mention a specific file, or / for commands.

readme.title: Generate a README
readme.desc: Read the project and write a README that matches its actual structure.

summarize.title: Summarize the docs
summarize.desc: List the documents and call out the open TODOs.

explore.title: Walk me through the project
explore.desc: Explain the folder layout and what each directory is for.

fix.title: Find what's broken
fix.desc: Scan for broken links, stale sections, and contradictions across files.

draft.title: Draft a new document
draft.desc: Start a fresh document from a topic — I'll pick the right structure.

git.title: What changed lately?
git.desc: Diff against the last commit and suggest doc updates that match.

footer.tagline: AI-native document workspace. The whole project becomes Claude context, with the files you already have as the source of truth.

hero.eyebrow: v0.1 · Filesystem as Source of Truth
hero.title: Your filesystem is
hero.subtitle: RabbitDocs turns the whole project into Claude context — the Markdown and Git you already have become the only source of truth your AI needs.

valueProps.title: Three equations that define our approach.

context.label: 01 / Context
context.title: Project = Context
context.description: Open a project and Claude Agent immediately sees all your Markdown and Git repo — no manual knowledge feeding.

truth.label: 02 / Truth
truth.title: Files = Truth
truth.description: Docs live as real files on the filesystem. Git-trackable, editable with any tool. No more black-box databases.

action.label: 03 / Action
action.title: Sandbox = Action
action.description: Selected code runs safely in an isolated cloud sandbox. Results flow back to the chat, closing the loop.

features.title: The complete capability matrix.
features.subtitle: Every piece of the AI collaboration puzzle lives in your filesystem.

thinking.title: Extended Thinking, visible
thinking.description: See Claude's reasoning unfold in real time. You don't just get answers — you audit the chain of thought.

mcp.title: MCP tool extensions
mcp.description: Plug in GitHub, Jira, Notion, or any MCP server. Expand AI capabilities on demand, no vendor lock-in.

sandbox.title: One-click cloud sandbox
sandbox.description: Code runs in an isolated environment, output flows back. AI is no longer a talking engineer.

chatToDoc.title: Chat-to-doc
chatToDoc.description: Save any chat as Markdown. Reverse direction: any doc can be Chat context.

passkey.title: Passkey login
passkey.description: WebAuthn modern auth. Skip password managers — log in with fingerprint or face.

usage.title: Transparent token usage
usage.description: TPM/RPM trends from 1h to 30d. See the real cost. Pick plans with confidence.

truth.title: Your Markdown is the only fact your AI sees.
truth.description: Traditional knowledge bases lock docs in databases — not Git-trackable, not VS Code-editable. RabbitDocs takes the other path: docs are .md files, and Claude Agent reads them directly as context.
truth.bullet1: Markdown docs are real files inside the project
truth.bullet2: Editable with any external tool, Git, or CI pipeline
truth.bullet3: Delete, move, rename are preserved in Git history

thinking.title: AI's reasoning, fully visible.
thinking.description: Extended Thinking is on by default. Before answering, Claude unfolds the files it read, the steps it took, and the options it weighed. You don't just see results — you audit reasoning.
thinking.bullet1: Thinking unfolds by default, can be collapsed
thinking.bullet2: Token usage broken down by phase
thinking.bullet3: Regenerate, follow-up, edit — chat is not one-way

testimonial1.quote: I used to copy-paste code to feed AI. Now Claude just sees it when I open the project. Filesystem as Source of Truth changed my workflow completely.
testimonial1.author: Chen M.

testimonial2.quote: Our 5-person team shares repos and MCP config via Workspace. New hires are productive on day one, and MCP tools are configured once for the whole team.
testimonial2.author: Lina K.

testimonial3.quote: The worst part of tech writing is docs that drift from code. Here, code that runs in chat can be saved as a doc. Source of truth stays aligned forever.
testimonial3.author: Yuki S.

cta.title: Stop fighting with the clipboard. Ship with context.
cta.subtitle: Start free, no credit card. 30 seconds to turn a project into Claude's workspace.

meta.title: Features
meta.description: Everything RabbitDocs does: Filesystem as Source of Truth, Claude Agent SDK, MCP tool extensions, cloud sandbox, Extended Thinking, and more.

features.title: A toolkit built for AI collaborators.
features.subtitle: From a single Markdown to an entire Git repo, from a local script to a cloud sandbox — {brand} puts everything AI collaboration needs in your filesystem.

context.title: Context
context.subtitle: Open a project and Claude Agent immediately sees all your Markdown and Git repo — no manual knowledge feeding.

context.title: Project-as-context
context.description: The whole project directory is Claude Agent's working context. Zero file-retrieval latency.

thinking.title: Extended Thinking, visible
thinking.description: Reasoning unfolds by default. Token usage broken down by phase.

usage.title: Transparent token usage
usage.description: TPM/RPM trends from 1h to 30d. Pick plans with real data.

truth.title: Truth
truth.subtitle: Docs are real files on the filesystem — Git-trackable, editable with any external tool.

fs.title: Filesystem as Source of Truth
fs.description: Markdown docs are real files inside the project. Editable with VS Code, Git, or any CI pipeline.

git.title: Git integration
git.description: Bind a Git repo. GitNexus auto-indexes the code graph for precise cross-branch lookup.

chatToDoc.title: Chat-to-doc
chatToDoc.description: Save any chat as Markdown. Reverse direction: any doc can be Chat context.

tools.title: Tools
tools.subtitle: AI capabilities expanded on demand. Code runs safely. Tool calls are fully audited.

mcp.title: MCP tool extensions
mcp.description: Plug in GitHub, Jira, Notion, or any MCP server. No ecosystem lock-in.

sandbox.title: One-click cloud sandbox
sandbox.description: Code runs in an isolated environment. Output flows back. Loop complete.

audit.title: Tool-call audit
audit.description: Every MCP call recorded, replayable, and traceable. Compliance-friendly.

collab.title: Collaboration
collab.subtitle: Teams share repos, MCP, and skills. New hires are productive on day one. Public links are content.

workspace.title: Workspace-level sharing
workspace.description: One repo, one MCP config, one set of skills — for the whole team.

share.title: Public HTML share
share.description: Any chat or doc generates a public link. Sandboxed iframe. No account required.

passkey.title: Passkey login
passkey.description: WebAuthn modern auth. Skip password managers. Login with fingerprint or face.

cta.title: Bring all of this home.
cta.subtitle: Start free, no credit card. 30 seconds to turn a project into Claude's workspace.

meta.title: Pricing
meta.description: Choose a plan that fits your team. Free for individuals, metered by token quota for teams.

pricing.title: Pay for what you use. Grow with your team.
pricing.subtitle: Free forever for individual developers. Teams pay for token quota and collaboration seats.

free.name: Personal
free.description: For indie devs and small projects

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: For power users and small teams

custom.title: Need bigger scale?
custom.description: Custom plans, dedicated support, and self-hosted deployment for teams of 50+.

items.question: Can I switch plans anytime?
items.answer: Yes. Upgrades and downgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing. No lock-in.

items.question: What happens when my token quota runs out?
items.answer: You'll get an email at 10% remaining. After that, chats pause but document editing keeps working. Quota resets on the 1st.

items.question: Do you offer self-hosted?
items.answer: Yes — Team and above can be self-hosted. Contact sales to scope environment and requirements.

items.question: Student or open-source discounts?
items.answer: Students with a .edu email can apply for Pro for free. Open-source projects get Team at a discount.

items.question: Where is my data? Can I export?
items.answer: Docs live as Markdown files in your filesystem. We never lock your data. db-dump exports all settings and history.

items.question: Refund policy?
items.answer: Full refund within 7 days of first subscription. After that, billed per used month. Contact support.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.subtitle: Free to begin, 30 seconds to onboard.

meta.title: Use cases
meta.description: See how different roles use RabbitDocs to ship better docs and collaborate faster.

useCases.title: Built for every kind of workflow.
useCases.subtitle: From indie devs to AI engineering teams, RabbitDocs can be your second brain.

solo.title: Indie developer
solo.description: Same context from side project to production. Stop losing information at handoff.
highlights: Just git clone a new project to start — no migration
Docs and code share the same source of truth
Local-first: keep working without a network

team.title: Small team (3-10)
team.description: Workspace sharing. New hires go from PR review to productive on day one.
highlights: Shared MCP tools — no repeated config per person

writer.title: Technical writer
writer.description: Code that runs in chat can be saved as a doc. Source of truth stays aligned forever.
highlights: Code examples and docs share the same source
Public HTML share for any doc

ai.title: AI engineer
ai.description: MCP extensions + cloud sandbox. Train/eval pipeline completes in one workspace.
highlights: Cloud sandbox safely runs any code

cta.title: Find the workflow that fits you.
cta.subtitle: 30 seconds to onboard. Every role benefits immediately.

meta.title: User Guide
meta.description: Learn how to use RabbitDocs — from AI chat to document management, projects, and team collaboration.

hero.title: User Guide
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of RabbitDocs.

signup.title: Sign Up & Log In

steps.step1: Click "Sign up" on the top right corner.
steps.step2: Enter your email and password. If an invite code is required, enter the one provided by your admin.
steps.step3: If email verification is enabled, click the link in your inbox.
steps.step4: You're in! Start a new chat or explore the app.

signup.passkey: Passkey login (if enabled) lets you sign in with fingerprint or face recognition — no password needed. Set it up in Settings after your first login.

chat.title: AI Chat
chat.subtitle: AI chat is the core of RabbitDocs. Open a project, and AI automatically sees all your files as context.

start.title: Start a conversation
start.desc: Click "New Chat" in the left sidebar, pick an AI model at the top, type your question, and press Enter. Responses stream in real time.

thinking.title: Extended Thinking
thinking.desc: AI's reasoning process is visible by default. You'll see which files it read, which options it weighed, and how it reached the conclusion — not just the final answer.

actions.title: Continue & regenerate
actions.desc: Ask follow-up questions to go deeper. Click regenerate if a response isn't satisfactory. Edit previous messages to refine your query.

projects.title: Projects & Files
projects.subtitle: A project is a folder of Markdown files — and the AI's context.
projects.create: Create a project from the sidebar, give it a name, and start adding files.
projects.files: Use the file tree to create files, folders, edit Markdown, or delete and rename items. All files are real filesystem files — editable with VS Code, tracked by Git.
projects.save: In any chat, click "Save to Document" to persist AI output as a file in your project. Saved files become context for future chats.

templates.title: Templates
templates.desc: Choose from 6 built-in templates (Meeting Notes, Project Proposal, PRD, Tech Design, Blog Post, Release Notes) to kickstart your document with AI assistance.

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.desc: Workspaces group related projects for team collaboration. Create a workspace, add projects, invite members — new hires get full project context on day one.

sharing.title: Share & Publish
sharing.docs: Share any document via a unique link. Recipients view it in their browser — no login needed.
sharing.chat: Share a conversation to let others see the full AI collaboration process and results.
sharing.revoke: Revoke sharing links at any time.

account.title: Account Settings
account.profile: Update your display name and avatar from the top-right menu.

models.title: My Models (BYOK)
models.desc: Add your own API key and model endpoint under "My Models". Your key is private — only you can use it.

billing.title: Subscription & Usage
billing.desc: View your plan, token usage trends (1h to 30d), and order history under "Billing". Quota resets on the 1st of each month. Chat pauses when quota runs out, but document editing keeps working.

items.q: How does AI know my document content?
items.a: When you open a project and start chatting, AI reads all files in that project as context. No copy-paste needed.
All documents are real Markdown files on the server filesystem. You can export them, manage with Git, or edit with any external tool.

items.q: Does AI modify my files automatically?
items.a: No. AI responses live in the chat. Files are only written when you explicitly click "Save to Document".
Available models depend on admin configuration. You can also add your own API key under "My Models".
Ask your admin for an invite code. Teammates register with the code, then you add them to your workspace.

items.q: What happens when token quota runs out?
items.a: You'll get an email at 10% remaining. Once depleted, chat pauses but document editing continues. Quota resets monthly.

items.q: Can I edit files outside RabbitDocs?
items.a: Yes. All files are standard Markdown. If your project has Git enabled, clone the repo, edit with VS Code, and sync back.

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.subtitle: Start free, no credit card required. Turn your project into an AI workspace in 30 seconds.

meta.title: About
meta.description: Meet the RabbitDocs team. Our mission, roadmap, and how to reach us.

about.title: Making the filesystem the only source of truth for the AI era.
about.subtitle: We believe the future of human-AI collaboration shouldn't be locked inside some SaaS black box. Your files, your Git, your workflow — all local, all yours.

mission.label: Mission
mission.title: Build a tool that respects the filesystem for human and AI collaborators.
mission.description: The past decade taught us to move everything into cloud SaaS. But in the AI era, agents don't need rows in a SaaS DB — they need real files in your filesystem. RabbitDocs wants to be that tool where AI and humans share the same truth.
bullets: Filesystem as Source of Truth — never lock your data
Local-first, cloud as just a collaboration channel
Open-source friendly, respect the existing toolchain

items.title: Filesystem integration + Claude Agent SDK
items.description: Whole project as AI context, Extended Thinking visible

items.title: ACP long-connection + Workspace collaboration
items.description: Persistent agent sessions, cross-project member management, token quota

items.title: Cloud sandbox GA + MCP tool marketplace
items.description: One-click code execution, official MCP server directory

items.title: Team edition GA + audit logs
items.description: SSO, audit logs, SCIM user provisioning

items.title: Mobile + offline-first
items.description: iOS / Android clients. Edit offline; sync when online.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: We want to hear from you.
contact.description: Product feedback, custom needs, or just want to talk about AI and filesystems? Reach out below.

cta.title: Want to build this with us?
cta.subtitle: We're hiring and looking for early users and partners.

integrationsPage.title: Integrations
integrationsPage.subtitle: Connect your AI tools and external services
integrationsPage.aiAssistantDesc: Let external AI tools (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.) connect to this system via MCP
integrationsPage.rabbitdocsDesc: Provide this system's document management capabilities to AI tools
integrationsPage.ideConfigDesc: Copy the configuration below to the corresponding tool's config file
integrationsPage.qoderFile: Location: IDE Settings → MCP → My Servers
integrationsPage.lovableStep2: Click Connectors in the left sidebar
integrationsPage.lovableStep3: Select Chat connectors → New MCP server
integrationsPage.lovableStep4: Enter the MCP Endpoint URL above as the Server URL
integrationsPage.lovableStep5: Select Bearer token as authentication, paste the API Key above
integrationsPage.boltStep2: Click + in the chatbox, then Connectors → Manage connectors
integrationsPage.boltStep4: Enter the MCP Endpoint URL above as the URL
integrationsPage.boltStep6: Select API key as Authentication, paste the API Key above
integrationsPage.v0Step2: Click Add MCP in the MCP Connections settings
integrationsPage.v0Step4: Enter the MCP Endpoint URL above as the Server URL
integrationsPage.v0Step5: Select Bearer Token as authentication, paste the API Key above
integrationsPage.replitStep2: Open MCP configuration in the editor
integrationsPage.replitStep4: Enter the MCP Endpoint URL above as the URL
integrationsPage.replitStep5: Add a custom header: key is "Authorization", value is "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
integrationsPage.workbuddyStep4: Enter the MCP Endpoint URL above as the Server URL
integrationsPage.workbuddyStep5: Paste the API Key above for authentication
integrationsPage.regenerateDesc: The old key will be invalidated immediately. Make sure to update all MCP client configurations.
integrationsPage.keyError: Failed to load MCP API key
integrationsPage.thirdPartyMcpDesc: Add third-party MCP servers for quick import into projects
integrationsPage.emptyDesc: Add your commonly used MCP servers to import them into projects with one click.
integrationsPage.moreDesc: More integrations coming soon, stay tuned
integrationsPage.feishuDesc: Feishu message notifications and document sync
integrationsPage.importExportDesc: Feishu document import and export sync
integrationsPage.importDesc: Select MCP services to import into the current project.
integrationsPage.importNoSelection: Please select at least one MCP
integrationsPage.importEmpty: No MCP available to import. Please add MCP servers in the Integrations page first.
integrationsPage.aiInstallDesc: Copy the prompt below and paste it into your AI chat to auto-configure
integrationsPage.aiPromptCopied: AI install prompt copied to clipboard

feedbackModal.title: Feedback
feedbackModal.submitSuccess: Feedback submitted successfully. Thank you for your input!
